programs.name: Medicaid for the Aged, Blind and Disabled (ABD)
programs.what_it_does: Provides medical coverage for seniors and disabled individuals meeting income and asset limits. In South Dakota, it uses federal asset limits such as $9,660 for single and $14,470 for married applicants.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+, blind, or disabled individuals with low income and assets.
programs.benefit_value: Health coverage including long-term services; home equity limit $752,000 for LTSS.[1]

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Options and Person Centered Excellence (HOPE) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services to allow enrollees to live in the community instead of nursing homes. Includes homemaker services, private duty nursing, adult day care, emergency response, meals, assisted living, and more.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Individuals 65+ or disabled needing nursing home level of care, with low income/assets and home equity under $752,000.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive HCBS to prevent institutionalization; covers specified services like nursing and assisted living.[1]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: No specific South Dakota name or details found in results; generally provides comprehensive medical and social services for frail seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Frail seniors 55+ eligible for nursing home care preferring community living.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Helps pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for eligible low-income seniors. South Dakota follows federal asset limits of $9,660 single/$14,470 married.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries with low income and assets meeting federal limits.
programs.benefit_value: Covers Part A/B premiums and cost-sharing based on program (QMB full, SLMB/QI partial).[1]

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides food benefits to low-income individuals including seniors. Administered through state DSS.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Monthly EBT benefits for groceries based on household size/income.

programs.name: Low Income Energy Assistance Program (LIEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Helps low-income South Dakotans pay home heating costs. Provides one-time payments during heating season.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Heating bill payment grants based on income, household size, and fuel type.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides weatherization services through Community Action Programs to improve energy efficiency for low-income homes. Includes insulation, sealing, and heating upgrades.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost home energy improvements.

programs.name: Senior Health Information and Insurance Education (SHIINE)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, unbiased, confidential counseling on Medicare, insurance, and related benefits. Funded by federal government.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and families needing Medicare/insurance guidance.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: No specific SD name in results; delivers meals to homebound seniors. Often through local senior centers or Aging programs.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals.

programs.name: Respite Care
programs.what_it_does: Provides temporary relief for caregivers of seniors/disabled through HOPE Waiver or other services. Includes in-home support like personal care.[7]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time community service jobs with training to develop employment skills. Helps unemployed seniors gain work experience.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed SD residents 55+ with income ≤125% federal poverty level.
programs.benefit_value: Part-time paid positions at non-profits/government agencies.

programs.name: Legal Services for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Offers legal aid and consumer protection for seniors, including scam prevention. Through Attorney General and legal aid programs.[6]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman
programs.what_it_does: No specific SD name in results; advocates for residents in long-term care facilities. Part of Adult Services and Aging.[6]

programs.name: Commodity Supplemental Food Program (CSFP) / Senior Box Program
programs.what_it_does: Distributes monthly boxes of nutritious, non-perishable food to supplement diets. Helps seniors afford medicine/utilities.[2]
